directors.name: Eliane Aleixo Lustosa de Andrade
directors.bio_summary: Ms. Aleixo Lustosa most recently served as Managing Director at the Brazilian Development Bank (BNDES), responsible for capital markets and the Brazilian Privatization Program. Earlier in her career she was Managing Director of several companies including LLX Logística S.A., Abril Group S.A., and Globex Utilidades S.A., and served as CIO of the Petrobras Employee Pension Fund. She holds a Ph.D. in Finance from Pontifical Catholic University of Rio de Janeiro.

directors.name: Carol Browner
directors.bio_summary: Ms. Browner is senior of counsel at Covington & Burling LLP in their ESG practice. She previously served as senior counsel at Albright Stonebridge Group (2011–2021) and as Assistant to President Obama and director of the White House Office of Energy and Climate Change Policy (2009–2011). Earlier she was Administrator of the EPA from 1993 to 2001.
prior_employers_named: White House Office of Energy and Climate Change Policy

directors.name: Gregory Heckman
directors.bio_summary: Mr. Heckman has been CEO of Bunge since 2019 and a director since October 2018, bringing over 40 years of experience in agriculture, energy and food processing. He is Founding Partner of Flatwater Partners and served as CEO of The Gavilon Group from 2008 to 2015. Prior to Gavilon, he served as COO of ConAgra Foods Commercial Products and President and COO of ConAgra Trade Group.

directors.name: Adrian Isman
directors.bio_summary: Mr. Isman has served as Chief Growth Officer of Calosense Inc. since January 2025 (and as CEO from November 2023 to December 2024). He spent the majority of his career at Louis Dreyfus Company (1985–1997 and 2000–2022), most recently as CEO North America Region from 2017 to 2022. Between stints at LDC he served as CEO of Marc Rich Agriculture Latin America from 1997 to 2000.

directors.name: Anne Jensen
directors.bio_summary: Ms. Jensen became Chief Operating Officer, Assets & Logistics for Norden, a global shipping company in Denmark, in January 2024. Previously she served as President and CEO of TotalEnergies EP Canada from 2021 to 2023, and held prior roles at Maersk Oil and Shell across multiple regions. She holds a Business Administration degree from Copenhagen Business School.

directors.name: Linda Jojo
directors.bio_summary: Ms. Jojo served as Executive Vice President and Chief Customer Officer for United Airlines from 2022 until her retirement in 2024, and previously as United's EVP of Technology and Chief Digital Officer from 2017 to 2022. Prior to United she was EVP and Chief Information Officer for Rogers Communications and held senior roles at Energy Future Holdings Corporation, Flowserve Corporation and General Electric.

directors.name: Christopher Mahoney
directors.bio_summary: Mr. Mahoney is the former CEO of Glencore Agriculture Ltd., where he worked for 21 years and served as CEO from 2016 until his retirement in 2019. He has served as non-executive director on the Viterra Ltd. board since 2019 and currently serves as non-executive Chairman of ED&F Man Holdings Ltd. Prior to Glencore, he worked at Cargill from 1982 to 1997.

directors.name: Monica McGurk
directors.bio_summary: Ms. McGurk is currently CEO of Glanbia Performance Nutrition Americas, which she joined in September 2024. Prior to that she served in executive leadership roles at Tropicana Brands Group (2022–2024) and held positions at Kellogg Company, Tyson Foods and The Coca-Cola Company. She holds a B.A. from Harvard University and an M.B.A. and M.A. from Stanford University.

directors.name: Kenneth Simril
directors.bio_summary: Mr. Simril is the former President and CEO of Fleischmann's Ingredients (2006–2021). Prior to that he was CFO and COO of Clipper Corporation and CFO of ClearPath Networks Inc., and served in finance and engineering roles at Mobil Oil Corporation and Exxon Mobil Corporation. He holds a B.S. in Petroleum Engineering from USC and an MBA from Harvard Business School.

directors.name: Markus Walt
directors.bio_summary: Mr. Walt is Head of Business Development for Glencore plc and a member of Glencore's Global Investment and Capital Allocation Committee, having begun his career at Glencore in 1999. He has been involved in major transactions including the Glencore IPO (2011) and acquisitions of Viterra (2012) and Xstrata (2013), and has served on the Viterra Board since 2016. He holds an MBA from the University of Zurich and is a CFA Charterholder.

directors.name: Henry "Jay" Winship
directors.bio_summary: Mr. Winship is Founder and Managing Member of Pacific Point Capital, LLC and Pacific Point Advisors, LLC. Prior to that, he was a Principal, Senior Managing Director and Member of the Investment Committee at Relational Investors, which he joined in 1996. He has over 25 years of experience as an institutional investor in investment management, accounting and financial management.

directors.name: Mark Zenuk
directors.bio_summary: Mr. Zenuk has served as Managing Partner of Tillridge Global Agribusiness Partners, an agribusiness private equity firm, since 2016. Previously he was a Managing Director at NGP Energy Capital Management (2010–2016) leading the agribusiness investment platform, and before that held many domestic and international executive leadership roles at Archer Daniels Midland Company. He has also served as General Manager of the Saskatchewan Wheat Pool and Marketing Manager for the Canadian Wheat Board.

extraction_notes: Tenure years are taken from the skills matrix table at the top of the director section ('Board Tenure (Years Completed)'). Four directors (Isman, Jensen, Mahoney, Walt) are contingent nominees pending the Viterra Transaction closing and have 0 tenure years. The proxy does not include a separate executive officers section in the extracted text, so executive_officers is an empty array. Other public board listings reflect only those explicitly stated as 'to Present' in each director's bio. CoreLogic, Inc. and At Home Group, Inc. were listed in the 5-year history but are not current, so they are excluded from other_public_boards. Linda Jojo's tenure is listed as 0 in the skills matrix (new nominee).
